# Feature: Save Keyboard Shortcut — Ctrl/Cmd+S in the Request Editor

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-010
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

`Ctrl+S` (Windows/Linux) / `Cmd+S` (macOS) in the request editor saves the active tab's request — the exact behavior of the existing save button: direct save when the request is collection-linked, otherwise open the Save wizard. Scoped to the request-editor container (not document-global) so Environments/Flows panes are not hijacked; they can adopt the same hook later.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No data. New hook contract:

### Entities & Attributes

- **`useSaveShortcut(onSave: () => void, options?: { enabled?: boolean })`** (new core hook, `packages/core/src/hooks/useSaveShortcut.ts`): returns a `React.KeyboardEventHandler` (e.g., `{ onKeyDown }`) to spread onto the scoping container. Match: `e.key.toLowerCase() === 's' && (e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey)` and not `e.altKey`/`e.shiftKey`; on match `e.preventDefault()` + `e.stopPropagation()` then `onSave()`. No-op when `enabled === false`.
- **Wiring target**: the existing save-click handler in each platform `RequestEditor.tsx` (vscode: the `folderPath`-gated handler at [RequestEditor.tsx:146-156](../../packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx) — direct `onSaveRequest` when collection-linked, else `setIsRequestSaveWizardOpen(true)`; web equivalent).

### Relationships

- Container-scoped listener → works only while focus is inside the request editor subtree (inputs, body editor, etc. all bubble to it).

### Indexes & Constraints

- vscode webview: keystrokes in a focused webview stay in the webview — no `package.json` keybinding contribution needed; verify no conflict with VS Code's own Ctrl+S (plan note).
- Browser default save dialog suppressed by `preventDefault`.

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/hooks/useSaveShortcut.ts` — NEW hook
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — export
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx` — attach to editor container
- `packages/web/src/RequestEditor.tsx` (web equivalent file) — attach
- `packages/core/src/test/hooks/useSaveShortcut.test.tsx` — NEW hook tests
- `docs/README.md` or `docs/features/requests.md` — new "Keyboard shortcuts" section (single entry, structured for growth)
- `.spec-lite/TODO.md` — enhancement entry: adopt `useSaveShortcut` in Environments/Flows panes

## 4. Dependencies

- None.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: `useSaveShortcut` core hook

- [ ] **Implementation**: Create the hook per §2 — pure, platform-agnostic (no `window`/`document` access; it only returns an event handler, complying with core's zero-platform-API rule). Memoize the handler with `useCallback` on `[onSave, enabled]`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: RTL with a wrapper div — Ctrl+S fires `onSave` once and the event is `defaultPrevented`; Cmd+S (metaKey) fires; plain `s` doesn't; Ctrl+Shift+S doesn't; `enabled: false` doesn't; uppercase `S` (caps/shift edge — assert chosen behavior: shift excluded) doesn't.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc with `@example` showing container attachment.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- useSaveShortcut` green.

### TASK-002: Wire into both request editors + docs

- [ ] **Implementation**: In both platform `RequestEditor.tsx` files, attach the hook's `onKeyDown` to the top-level editor container and pass the existing save handler (the same function the save button calls — vscode [RequestEditor.tsx:146-156](../../packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx); locate the web counterpart). Disable while the save wizard is already open (`enabled: !isRequestSaveWizardOpen`) to avoid double-opening. Append the Environments/Flows adoption idea to `.spec-lite/TODO.md` (`- [ ] Adopt useSaveShortcut in Environments/Flows panes (discovered during: FEAT-010)`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: RTL on the editor — Ctrl+S with a collection-linked tab calls `onSaveRequest` with the active tab id; Ctrl+S with an unsaved tab opens the Save wizard; Ctrl+S while the wizard is open does nothing extra.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add a "Keyboard shortcuts" section (table: shortcut → action; single `Ctrl/Cmd+S` row) in `docs/features/requests.md`, linked from `docs/README.md`.
- **Verify**: vscode: focus the URL input, Ctrl+S — request saves (linked) or wizard opens (unsaved), and VS Code's own save-dialog does **not** trigger; web: browser save dialog suppressed, same behavior.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: Delegates entirely to the existing save flow (already `Result`-checked with notifications).
- **Logging**: N/A.
